description: CSS adjustments for sidebar left reserves, Legend button in header with icon tooltip, Luna no-placement popup fix, and dual-type monster reserve count fix
steps: 1. In src/App.css, find the `.reserve-item` rule and change padding to `0.15rem 0.15rem` and font-size to `0.65rem`. Find the `.player-reserves` rule and change gap to `0rem`.

2. In src/components/GameBoard.tsx, add a Legend button (round, 2px gold border, bold question mark '?' in the center) in the game-header, positioned to the right side BEFORE the mandate-counter div (Ronda: 1/3). On hover, show a vertical tooltip that lists ALL icons used in the game with their text names (Bushi, Shinto, Fortaleza, Daimyo, Monstruo, Moneda, PV, Honor, Ronin, Fuerza, Primavera, Verano, Otono, Invierno). The tooltip design must match the clan-tooltip styling from the home page (dark gradient background, gold border with gradient, rounded corners, shadow). Icons should all be the same size (around 20px, similar to map troop icons). Import all needed icons from ./Icons.

3. Add corresponding CSS styles for the legend button and tooltip in src/App.css. The button should be a small round circle (~28px), with 2px solid gold border (use var(--accent-gold) or the gold gradient), centered '?' in bold visible font. The tooltip should appear on hover, positioned below the button, with a vertical layout, gold gradient border (matching clan-tooltip border style), dark background gradient, each row showing icon + text label. Add appropriate z-index so it appears above everything.

4. In src/store/gameStore.ts, fix the Luna monster no-placement detection. Currently in `doBuySeasonCard` (around line 600), for non-Komainu monsters, the code sets `monsterPlacementPopupVisible: true` without checking Luna's valid provinces. Add the Luna check BEFORE setting the popup visible: if the player is Luna clan and has no valid province (same logic as in confirmMonsterPlacement - iterate provinces for fortress + non-fortress figures < 2), then skip the placement popup entirely, increment player.monsters by 1, and set `monsterNoPlacementPopupVisible: true` instead (also set `monsterPlacementCard: boughtCard` so the name is available).

5. In src/components/GameBoard.tsx, update the Monster No Placement Popup to display the monster name. Change from `

{t('monster.noPlacement')}

` to a two-line display: first line 'No puedes sacar a (Monster Name),' and second line 'ira directo a tu reserva'. Use the `monsterPlacementCard` from the store to get the name. Update the i18n keys accordingly: add 'monster.noPlacementLine1' and 'monster.noPlacementLine2' (or use a format with {name} interpolation).

6. Update src/i18n/en.ts and src/i18n/es.ts with the new translation keys for the no-placement popup text: es: line1='No puedes sacar a {name},' line2='ira directo a tu reserva'; en: line1='You cannot deploy {name},' line2='it will go directly to your reserve'.

7. Fix the dual-type monster reserve count in src/store/gameStore.ts. When the Luna no-placement flow sends a monster to reserve (incrementing player.monsters), ALSO check if the monster is a dual-type: if it's sp-komainu or su-hotei, also increment player.shinto by 1; if it's su-yurei or sp-fukurokuju, set player.hasDaimyo = true. WAIT - actually looking at the PlayerPanel code more carefully, the reserve display uses `computeReserveTotals` which computes effective totals based on cards owned vs deployed on map. The player.shinto/player.bushi/etc fields represent the ACTUAL reserve figure counts, not card-based counts. The dual-type bonus in PlayerPanel is already computed from `monsterCardsInReserve`. So the issue is likely in how player.monsters is managed. When a monster goes to reserve via Luna no-placement, the code already increments player.monsters. The PlayerPanel then separately checks which monster cards are NOT on map and adds their shinto/daimyo bonuses. HOWEVER, if the monster was never placed on the map, it's already in monsterCardsInReserve naturally. So the real issue may be that player.monsters is being incremented (which makes it show in the monster count) but the card-based computation for shinto might be excluding it. Actually re-reading the code: `notOnMap` filters cards that are NOT deployed. If a monster was never placed, it won't be in `deployedMonsterCardIds`. So it WILL be in `notOnMap`. Then `monsterCardsInReserve` = `notOnMap` (unless there's a komainu at temple adjustment). Then `shintoMonstersInReserve` counts sp-komainu and su-hotei in that list. This should work. The issue might be that when a monster goes directly to reserve without being placed, it may not be properly tracked. Let me check: when `confirmMonsterPlacement` sends to reserve with `monsters: p.monsters + 1`, it doesn't advance train resolution immediately - that happens in `dismissMonsterNoPlacement`. The card is already in `seasonCards` (added by `buySeasonCard`). So when computing reserves, the card IS in seasonCards, it's NOT in deployedMonsterCardIds (never placed), so it IS in notOnMap. The player.monsters count is incremented. The authoritative reserve is player.monsters. And notOnMap.length should equal player.monsters (unless komainu at temple). So the shinto count from shintoMonstersInReserve should work. Unless there's a timing issue where the state hasn't been updated yet. Actually wait - look at the regular placement flow: when doPlaceMonster places a monster on the map, it does NOT decrement player.monsters! So player.monsters stays at whatever it was set to. For monsters placed on the map via normal flow (non-Luna), player.monsters is never incremented at all, because the normal flow goes: buy card -> show popup -> confirmMonsterPlacement -> doPlaceMonster (places figure on map). The monsters field is only incremented in the Luna no-placement case. So for a NORMAL monster placement (placed on map), player.monsters = 0, and the monster figure is on the map. If another monster goes to reserve via Luna, player.monsters = 1. The monstersInReserve = player.monsters = 1. But monsterCards.length = 2 (both cards owned). notOnMap = cards not in deployedMonsterCardIds = 1 card. Then check: notOnMap.length (1) > player.monsters (1)? No, equal. So no komainu-at-temple adjustment. monsterCardsInReserve = notOnMap = [the card that went to reserve]. If that card is sp-komainu or su-hotei, shintoMonstersInReserve = 1. So effectiveShintoReserve = player.shinto + 1. This seems correct. BUT WAIT - for Komainu specifically, it goes through a DIFFERENT flow (komainuChoice). If Komainu is chosen for 'map' and Luna has no space, the flow goes through confirmMonsterPlacement which handles the Luna check. But for other monsters like su-hotei, they go through the normal popup flow. The BUG is: when doBuySeasonCard shows the monsterPlacementPopupVisible for Luna (before the fix), the player clicks Accept, THEN confirmMonsterPlacement runs, detects no valid province, increments monsters, and shows monsterNoPlacementPopupVisible. The shinto/daimyo counts should still be computed correctly by PlayerPanel since the card is in seasonCards and not deployed. So maybe the bug is actually that the user buys Komainu and the POPUP flow is wrong (showing placement popup instead of no-space popup). After fixing step 4, the reserve computation should be fine because the card stays in seasonCards and is computed as 'in reserve' by PlayerPanel. Let me focus on ensuring the FIX in step 4 is correct and the PlayerPanel computation is already handling dual-types correctly. If after the fix, testing still shows wrong counts, we may need to debug further. For now, verify the PlayerPanel computeReserveTotals function is correct and add a note to verify.
acceptance_criteria: The .reserve-item CSS has padding: 0.15rem 0.15rem and font-size: 0.65rem
A round Legend button with gold border and '?' appears in the header before Ronda: 1/3
Hovering the Legend button shows a vertical tooltip with all game icons and their labels, styled like the clan tooltip with gold gradient border
For Luna clan, when buying a non-Komainu monster with no valid province, the placement popup does NOT appear - only the no-space popup appears
The no-space popup text shows two lines: 'No puedes sacar a [MonsterName],' and 'ira directo a tu reserva'
The project builds without errors (npx vite build)
The PlayerPanel correctly shows dual-type monster bonuses in the shinto and daimyo reserve counts
verification: Run `npx vite build` in the project root and confirm zero errors
findings: All changes build cleanly. The Luna no-placement fix was added to doBuySeasonCard for the non-Komainu else branch. The Komainu path already goes through confirmMonsterPlacement which has its own Luna check. The PlayerPanel computeReserveTotals function correctly computes dual-type monster bonuses (shinto from sp-komainu/su-hotei, daimyo from su-yurei/sp-fukurokuju) based on cards in reserve vs deployed. The no-placement popup now requires monsterPlacementCard to be truthy (added to conditional), which is always set in both code paths.
